interfaces.name: TableProcessor
interfaces.signalFlowRole: Lookup table step in signal path - maps pitch wheel input through a user-defined curve when UseTable is enabled

parameterGroups.label: Pitch Wheel Response
parameterGroups.rationale: These parameters control how the raw pitch wheel value is transformed: inversion flips the direction, and the table provides a custom mapping curve

parameters.name: Inverted
parameters.description: Inverts the modulation signal so pitch bend up produces lower values

parameters.name: UseTable
parameters.description: Enables a lookup table for custom pitch wheel response curves

parameterGroups.label: Smoothing
parameterGroups.rationale: Controls the temporal smoothing applied to the pitch wheel signal to reduce stepping artifacts from discrete MIDI values

parameters.name: SmoothTime
parameters.description: Smoothing time in milliseconds to reduce stepping on fast pitch wheel movements

gaps.question: How is the 14-bit MIDI pitch bend value (0-16383) mapped to the 0-1 modulation range? Is center position (8192) mapped to 0.5?
gaps.context: Users need to know the exact mapping to understand what modulation values correspond to pitch bend positions - especially whether center = 0.5 or some other convention

gaps.question: What is the exact processing order? Specifically: MIDI pitch bend -> normalize -> invert -> table lookup -> smooth, or a different order?
gaps.context: The pseudo-code must show the correct transformation chain from raw pitch bend to final modulation value

gaps.question: Where does the TableProcessor lookup occur in the signal path? Does it receive the normalized (and possibly inverted) value, or the raw normalized value before inversion?
gaps.context: The table input domain determines what the X axis represents to the user

gaps.question: What smoothing algorithm is used (exponential, linear ramp, low-pass filter)? Is smoothing applied per-sample or per-block?
gaps.context: The smoothing type affects the feel of pitch wheel response and is relevant for performance considerations

gaps.question: Does inversion use 1.0 - value, or does it mirror around center (0.5)? This matters because pitch bend is bipolar.
gaps.context: For a bipolar controller, simple 1-x inversion and center-mirroring have very different musical results

notes: TimeVariantModulator with MIDI input type 'none' per inference table, but the module clearly reads pitch bend MIDI messages. The inference table 'none' means no noteOn/noteOff dependency - the module still receives all MIDI via the general MIDI callback. Natural seeAlso candidates: MidiController (similar structure, different MIDI source), Aftertouch.
